input: hi. now its at 124 MILLION. i need more updates!! every category, guests, milestones, when each was hit and more

output.executive_summary: The 'Diss na raka' charity fundraiser, organized by influencer Łatwogang for Fundacja Cancer Fighters, concluded its nine-day continuous livestream on April 26, 2026, achieving historic success in Polish online fundraising. While you mentioned a total of 124 million PLN, this figure is unverified by primary sources. The highest credible media-reported total, reflecting the peak of the live on-stream counter, was approximately 122 million PLN. This live counter aggregated gross donations from multiple channels, including Siepomaga, Tipply, and YouTube. In contrast, the official Siepomaga platform's public snapshot showed a total of around 24 million PLN, representing the funds processed directly through that specific channel at a point in time. The campaign was marked by numerous celebrity guest appearances, on-stream stunts like head-shaving, and significant corporate donations, culminating in a record-breaking, though complex, financial outcome pending final audited reconciliation.
output.verification_status_of_124m_claim: The claim that the 'Diss na raka' fundraiser reached a total of 124 million PLN is currently unverified by any authoritative primary sources. Research across official channels, including the Siepomaga platform and statements from Fundacja Cancer Fighters, did not yield any confirmation of this specific amount. The highest reported figures in credible media outlets peaked at around 120-122 million PLN. The 124 million PLN figure likely originates from one or more of the following: misreporting in less formal media or social media posts, confusion with rapidly changing live counters, or the aggregation of gross, un-settled pledges that did not account for processing fees, potential chargebacks, or revenue-sharing agreements on platforms like YouTube.
output.fundraiser_total_analysis: There is a significant and expected discrepancy between the various reported fundraising totals for the 'Diss na raka' campaign. The highest figures, such as the media-reported peak of approximately 122 million PLN, reflect the on-stream live counter. This counter was an aggregator, displaying a gross, real-time sum of donations from multiple channels simultaneously: the official Siepomaga 'skarbonka', the Tipply donation link, YouTube Super Chats/Stickers, and direct corporate pledges announced on stream. This live total did not account for payment processor fees, platform revenue shares (e.g., YouTube's ~30% cut of Super Chat revenue), potential refunds or chargebacks, or settlement delays (e.g., Tipply's 15-day hold). In contrast, the much lower official Siepomaga platform total of 24,052,004 PLN represents only the funds processed and recorded directly through the Siepomaga website at a specific point in time. The final, audited total, which will be a net figure after all channels are settled and fees are deducted, is expected to be published by Fundacja Cancer Fighters at a later date and will likely differ from both the live counter peak and the standalone Siepomaga snapshot.

highest_media_reported_total.reporting_media: Bankier.pl (updated report), with corroborating reports of 'ponad 120 mln zł' from Onet/Plejada and Rzeczpospolita.

milestone_timeline.trigger_event: This milestone was achieved due to the early viral momentum of the 9-day stream, growing celebrity attention, and a specific pledge from Robert Lewandowski to post a TikTok video once this threshold was crossed.

milestone_timeline.date_achieved: During the ramp-up days of the stream, reported around 2026-04-24
milestone_timeline.trigger_event: Streamer Katarzyna 'Kasix' Paciorek shaved her head live on stream as a pledged stunt when the counter reached this amount.

This milestone was attributed to sustained activity, including guest appearances from celebrities like Bedoes, Doda, Julia Wieniawa, and Tomasz Karolak, as well as the viral reconciliation of influencers Wardęga, Friz, and Wersow.
Continuing celebrity guest appearances and pledges, including a promise from Julia Wieniawa for an event if 40M PLN was reached, contributed to this milestone.

A significant surge in donations linked to a wave of celebrity visits and head-shaving gestures by figures such as Julia 'Maffashion' Kuczyńska, Edyta Pazura, Katarzyna Nosowska, and Aleksandra Domańska, plus high-value corporate donations.
Author Blanka Lipińska shaved her head as a symbolic stunt once the counter reached this approximate milestone.

This major threshold was crossed as a result of the campaign's peak momentum, fueled by a continuous cascade of celebrity acts, reconciliations, and significant corporate donations from companies like Budimex, Tymbark, and XTB.

The reported peak of the fundraiser, achieved near the campaign's conclusion. This was the culmination of all previous events, including numerous high-profile guests, multiple celebrity head shaves, and substantial corporate gifts from donors like zen.com, Wydawnictwo Niezwykłe, and Allegro.

notable_guest_appearances.segment_description: Połączył się zdalnie ze streamem, gratulując organizatorom. Zobowiązał się do opublikowania swojego pierwszego od lipca 2025 roku filmiku na TikToku z piosenką „Diss na raka”, jeśli zbiórka przekroczy 10 milionów złotych. Obietnicę spełnił następnego dnia. Dodatkowo zaoferował podpisane koszulki FC Barcelona na aukcję charytatywną.
notable_guest_appearances.reported_impact: Jego pojawienie się i obietnica są bezpośrednio powiązane w doniesieniach medialnych z przekroczeniem progu 10 milionów złotych, co stanowiło ważny kamień milowy i katalizator dla dalszych darowizn i uwagi publicznej.

notable_guest_appearances.segment_description: Pojawiła się zdalnie, wykonując serię telefonów na żywo do innych celebrytów, takich jak Maryla Rodowicz, Zenek Martyniuk i Magda Gessler, aby zachęcić ich do wsparcia akcji i przekazywania darowizn. Jej segment był opisywany jako maraton telefoniczny mający na celu mobilizację wsparcia od znanych osobistości.
notable_guest_appearances.reported_impact: Segmenty z jej udziałem są opisywane w mediach jako momenty generujące gwałtowne wzrosty uwagi i zaangażowania widzów. Miała również symbolicznie pogodzić się z Magdą Gessler.

notable_guest_appearances.segment_description: W geście solidarności z pacjentami onkologicznymi, Kasix publicznie ogoliła głowę na żywo podczas transmisji. Był to zaplanowany element akcji, który miał zostać zrealizowany po osiągnięciu określonego progu finansowego.
notable_guest_appearances.reported_impact: Jej działanie nastąpiło po przekroczeniu progu 13 milionów złotych i było szeroko komentowane jako jeden z najbardziej poruszających i symbolicznych momentów transmisji, przyciągając dużą uwagę mediów i widzów.

notable_guest_appearances.segment_description: Historyczny moment dla polskiego YouTube, w którym długo skonfliktowani twórcy spotkali się na żywo w studio, aby publicznie się pogodzić. Po pojednaniu Wersow symbolicznie ogoliła głowę Sylwestrowi Wardędze.
notable_guest_appearances.reported_impact: To wydarzenie jest określane jako jeden z kluczowych czynników, które doprowadziły do ogromnego wzrostu oglądalności i darowizn, bezpośrednio przyczyniając się do przekroczenia progu 36 milionów złotych na liczniku na żywo.

notable_guest_appearances.segment_description: Pojawił się w studio razem ze swoimi serialowymi synami, Mateuszem Pawłowskim i Adamem Zdrójkowskim. Wspólnie wykonali polskie przeboje oraz akustyczną wersję piosenki przewodniej z serialu 'Rodzinka.pl'.
notable_guest_appearances.reported_impact: Ich występ był częścią segmentu, który przyciągnął dużą publiczność. Dołączyła do nich Julia Wieniawa, która rzuciła wyzwanie związane z osiągnięciem progu 40 milionów złotych.

notable_guest_appearances.segment_description: Dołączyła na streamie do obsady 'Rodzinka.pl'. Zaproponowała wyzwanie dla widzów: jeśli licznik zbiórki osiągnie 40 milionów złotych, zorganizuje 'piżama party' z pełną obsadą serialu.
notable_guest_appearances.reported_impact: Jej wyzwanie stanowiło kolejny cel motywujący widzów do wpłat w końcowej fazie transmisji i zostało zrealizowane, gdy licznik na żywo przekroczył 40 milionów.

notable_guest_appearances.segment_description: Jako współtwórca piosenki „Ciągle tutaj jestem (diss na raka)”, był centralną postacią akcji. Pojawiał się na streamie, wspierał organizatorów i sam dokonał znacznych wpłat. We wczesnej fazie streamu ogolił głowę i zrobił tatuaż na żywo.
notable_guest_appearances.reported_impact: Jego zaangażowanie było fundamentalne dla całej akcji. Doniesienia medialne wskazują, że jego całkowita darowizna wyniosła około 1 miliona złotych, co czyni go jednym z największych indywidualnych darczyńców.

celebrity_reconciliations.participants: Sylwester Wardęga, Friz (Karol Wiśniewski) i Wersow (Weronika Sowa)
celebrity_reconciliations.description: Podczas transmisji na żywo 25 kwietnia doszło do publicznego zakończenia długotrwałego konfliktu między czołowymi polskimi influencerami. Wardęga, Friz i Wersow spotkali się w studio, gdzie oficjalnie się pogodzili i podali sobie ręce. Kulminacyjnym i symbolicznym momentem było ogolenie głowy Wardęgi przez Wersow na wizji.
celebrity_reconciliations.reported_impact: Pojednanie jest szeroko opisywane jako historyczny moment w polskim internecie, który wygenerował ogromny wzrost oglądalności i napływ darowizn. Media bezpośrednio łączą to wydarzenie z przekroczeniem progu 36 milionów złotych na liczniku na żywo.

celebrity_reconciliations.description: W trakcie jednego ze swoich segmentów, Doda zadzwoniła na żywo do Magdy Gessler. Ich rozmowa została przedstawiona w mediach jako symboliczne pojednanie lub zażegnanie dawnych nieporozumień. Był to element szerszej akcji, w której Doda mobilizowała inne gwiazdy do wsparcia zbiórki.
celebrity_reconciliations.reported_impact: Wydarzenie to, podobnie jak inne telefony Dody, przyczyniło się do wzrostu zainteresowania streamem i było częścią strategii angażowania znanych osobistości w celu zwiększenia darowizn.

live_performances_and_concerts.performance_description: Aktor wraz z Mateuszem Pawłowskim i Adamem Zdrójkowskim (serialowi synowie) zaśpiewał na żywo kilka polskich przebojów. Ich występ zwieńczyło akustyczne wykonanie piosenki przewodniej z popularnego serialu 'Rodzinka.pl'.
Raper pojawił się w studio i dał krótki występ na żywo, wykonując swoje popularne utwory, w tym covery „Lady Pank” oraz piosenkę „Zwykły chłopak”.
Raper pojawił się na streamie 24 kwietnia. Doniesienia medialne wskazywały również, że w ramach akcji zaplanowany był jego mini-koncert, który miał odbyć się w piątek podczas trwania transmisji.

Utwór „Ciągle tutaj jestem (diss na raka)” był centralnym i nieprzerwanym elementem muzycznym całej 9-dniowej transmisji. Jego ciągłe odtwarzanie stanowiło podstawę wyzwania podjętego przez Łatwoganga i było głównym motywem całej akcji charytatywnej.

key_fundraising_mechanisms.flow_description: Donors make payments on the Siepomaga 'skarbonka' (collection box) page for the campaign. The funds are processed by payment service providers (PSPs) like PayU and Dotpay. The collected funds are legally held by Fundacja Siepomaga, which then executes a donation transfer to the beneficiary organization, Fundacja Cancer Fighters. The campaign page states that all funds go directly to the target organization.
key_fundraising_mechanisms.fee_structure: Fundacja Siepomaga applies a 6% allocation from the collected funds to cover its statutory goals and portal operating costs. In addition, PSPs and banks charge their own transaction processing fees (e.g., for card payments, currency conversions), which are deducted from the gross donation amount.
key_fundraising_mechanisms.settlement_timeline: There is no fixed public timeline. Fundacja Siepomaga executes transfers to the beneficiary foundation after a transfer request is made and internal verification is complete. In practice, this process can take from several days to a few weeks, depending on the verification process.

output.campaign_finale_summary: The 'Diss na raka' / 'Ciągle tutaj jestem (diss na raka)' campaign's nine-day continuous livestream was scheduled to conclude on Sunday, April 26, 2026, at 16:00 local Polish time, and media reports confirm it ended within this advertised window. The final day was marked by significant last-hour donation surges, with live counters showing rapid, high-volume increases. Media outlets reported escalating totals throughout the day; for instance, RMF reported a total of approximately 84 million PLN early on April 26th, while TVN24 reported that the fundraiser had surpassed the 100 million PLN mark by midday. This surge was driven by intense final appeals and a series of capstone programming segments. The finale featured milestone-triggered incentives and stunts, including solidarity head-shavings by celebrities like Edyta Pazura, Maffashion, and Kasix, as well as live mini-concerts and appearances by prominent artists and influencers such as Bedoes, Doda, and Szpaku, all designed to maximize engagement and donations in the closing hours.
output.post_campaign_communications: Immediately following the finale of the livestream on April 26, 2026, both the organizer Łatwogang and the beneficiary, Fundacja Cancer Fighters, engaged in public communications. Fundacja Cancer Fighters posted several thank-you messages and milestone updates on its social media channels, such as Instagram, celebrating interim totals as they were crossed. Similarly, Łatwogang and participating guests used their social platforms and gave media interviews to express gratitude and report on the preliminary success. A critical aspect of these post-finale communications was the clear distinction made between the preliminary totals displayed on live counters (which aggregated multiple channels like Siepomaga and Tipply) and the final, audited outcome. It was widely acknowledged that the figures reported by media (such as over 100 million PLN) were based on these live, gross counters. No consolidated, audited, or reconciled final financial statement was immediately published by Fundacja Cancer Fighters or an independent auditor. The public record consisted of these preliminary figures, with the understanding that a definitive, settled total would be announced at a later date after all donation channels were reconciled and funds were formally transferred.

records_and_benchmarks.record_claimed: Guinness World Record for 'Most money raised during a charity live broadcast'.
records_and_benchmarks.details: The Polish media outlet TVP reported that the 'Diss na raka' livestream had broken a Guinness World Record for the most money raised during a charity live broadcast. The report noted that the final figure would be subject to official verification by Guinness after the stream's conclusion. As of the research date, no independent, public confirmation from Guinness World Records has been located. Beyond this specific claim, the campaign is widely regarded as having set a new benchmark for online charity events in Poland, achieving massive scale in terms of participation (over 489,000 supporters on the Siepomaga platform alone), media reach, and live viewership, with reports of concurrent viewers peaking above 150,000.

controversies_and_criticisms.description: A significant controversy emerged involving the Polish fast-food chain Pasibus. The company publicly pledged to donate 60,000 PLN to the fundraiser on the condition that the popular singer Doda, a guest on the stream, agreed to perform at their festival. This conditional offer immediately drew significant public backlash from viewers and from Doda herself, who criticized the move as making charitable aid contingent on receiving promotional benefits. The incident was widely seen as an attempt at 'marketing-driven charity' and was rejected on-air by both Doda and the stream's organizer, Łatwogang, who emphasized that help should be unconditional.
controversies_and_criticisms.involved_parties: Pasibus (fast-food chain), Doda (singer), Łatwogang (stream organizer), Viewers/Public
controversies_and_criticisms.resolution_or_outcome: Following the widespread public criticism, Pasibus issued an apology on its social media channels for the conditional nature of their offer. While the company initially made a smaller payment of 10,000 PLN, it was later reported by Łatwogang that Pasibus had ultimately contributed a total of 100,000 PLN to the fundraiser. This larger, unconditional donation was widely interpreted as a damage control measure in response to the negative publicity. The incident sparked a broader conversation about the ethics of corporate donations and the importance of unconditional support in charity campaigns.

outputBasis.reasoning: Post-finale communications centered on acknowledging milestone milestones and clarifying how totals were reported. Public communications from Cancer Fighters and Latwogang highlighted that the live on-stream counter showed gross, multi-channel totals while the official, audited final total would come later after settlement and reconciliation across Siepomaga and Tipply. Journalistic roundups and interviews consistently frame these communications as public acknowledgments of progress and as caveats about final accounting. For example, articles recount that the live stream had surpassed major thresholds and that media outlets reported “over 100 million PLN” while the official platform totals remained lower, emphasizing the distinction between live gross counts and audited final numbers. Reports describe public thank-yous and milestone updates on social media as the campaign wound down, illustrating a transition from live-driven fundraising momentum to post-campaign communications that set expectations for an audited settlement. The cited sources also confirm that notable guests and moments continued to be referenced in post-finale coverage, reinforcing that communications continued to shape public understanding of the outcome and the next steps for final accounting. Direct quotes and paraphrased content from the sources illustrate the ongoing messaging: gratitude and milestone recognition on social platforms; explicit notes that the final, audited total would be announced later; and references to multi-channel aggregation and related fees that affect the comparison between live counters and platform totals.

citations.title: Łatwogang zebrał już ponad 100 mln zł. Nie tylko Blanka ...
excerpts: 26 kwietnia kwota przekroczyła już ponad 120 mln zł.

Od kilku dni setki tysięcy Polaków śledzi niezwykły, dziewięciodniowy stream charytatywny prowadzony przez influencera Łatwoganga, który powoli dobiega końca. Akcja, której celem jest wsparcie dzieci chorych na nowotwory, już przyniosła ponad 100 mln zł. W przedsięwzięcie zaangażowały się największe gwiazdy polskiej sceny muzycznej i sportowej.

outputBasis.reasoning: Direct mentions of guest appearances are the strongest support for the field value. The most relevant excerpts explicitly name guests, their actions on the stream, and the timing relative to milestones. For example, excerpts describing the in-studio reconciliation and head-shaving moments tied to specific guests (such as Wardęga, Friz, and Wersow) demonstrate the concrete presence of notable guests and their impact on audience engagement and donations. Other excerpts confirm high-profile appearances by Lewandowski, Kasix, and Julia Wieniawa, including their dates and the nature of their involvement (e.g., Lewandowski joining the stream and discussing a TikTok post tied to a fundraising milestone; Kasix shaving her head in solidarity after a milestone; Julia Wieniawa joining the Rodzinka.pl cast and motivating a milestone). Additional excerpts corroborate broader guest participation (e.g., Doda making calls to celebrities, Tomasz Karolak appearing with Rodzinka.pl cast, Michał Pol shaving head). Taken together, these excerpts create a robust set of data points about who appeared, when, and what impact their appearance had on fundraising momentum. The entries also help establish a timeline of guest-driven milestones, which is essential for populating a tracker’s notable_guest_appearances field with time-stamped, role-specific guest events. The most impactful moments are those where well-known figures publicly engaged on the livestream and are repeatedly referenced across multiple sources, reinforcing the connection between presence and fundraising dynamics.

excerpts: . W pewnym momencie firma Pasibus napisała, że jeśli artystka zgodzi się wystąpić na ich festiwalu, to wpłacą na zbiórkę Łatwoganga

outputBasis.reasoning: The strongest evidence of controversy is the explicit description of a conditional donation offer by Pasibus, which was to donate 60,000 PLN only if Doda performed at Pasibus’ festival. This is directly the central claim of the controversy, and it is followed by public backlash from Doda and viewers, labeling the offer as ‘marketing-driven charity’ and prompting calls for unconditional charity. The excerpts also document the on-air rejection by Doda and by the stream organizer, which underscores the ethical dispute at the heart of the controversy. In subsequent coverage, Pasibus apologizes for the conditional nature of the donation and the reports indicate an escalation to a larger unconditional donation, signaling a resolution and the community’s reception to the controversy. Together, these pieces support the field value’s controversy title (Pasibus Conditional Donation), its description (the conditional donation, public backlash, and critique of using charity for marketing), the involved parties (Pasibus, Doda, the streamer Patryk “Latwogang”, and public observers), and the stated resolution/outcome (apology and eventual unconditional donation). The sequence of claims is corroborated by multiple outlets, strengthening the linkage between the described events and the field value.

outputBasis.reasoning: The most relevant excerpt directly cites a Bankier report stating a figure of 122 million PLN, which precisely aligns with the requested value and timestamp. This provides the strongest evidence for the exact reported total and its media source. Related excerpts acknowledge high totals in the same ballpark (around 120 million PLN) from other credible outlets (Onet, Plejada) and later updates that the tally exceeded 120 million, thereby corroborating the trajectory toward the 122M mark and providing multi-source confirmation. The Bankier-based item is the anchor for the precise value in the field, while the companion excerpts from other outlets establish a consistent, cross-checked progression toward that milestone and offer a broader media context around the same period. The combination of a specific 122M Bankier update and corroborating 120M+ reports from other outlets best maps to the requested field value and its timestamp, making these excerpts the most relevant. The other excerpts noting counts around 100M or 105M serve as contextual milestones showing prior progression but are less directly aligned with the exact 122M figure.

outputBasis.reasoning: The claim to verify concerns the exact latest counter total for the fundraiser. The most directly relevant items are those that quote specific totals and timestamps from credible outlets. A publicly reported figure of 122 million PLN is cited as a milestone in multiple outlets, and a concrete timestamped entry notes 122 million as of a published date/time. This supports the idea that 124 million is not yet confirmed as an official total, since the nearest publicly corroborated figures sit at or just above 120 million but below 124 million, and Siepomaga’s platform totals show a lower, authoritative figure (around 24 million PLN at an earlier checkpoint). The evidence that live-stream counters exceeded 100 million and then 120+ million provides necessary context for why the 124 million figure could appear in some reports or social chatter, but those sources often rely on live counter displays that aggregate multiple streams and may include unsettled contributions and fees, which are not part of the Siepomaga total. Therefore, the strongest, verifiable point is that an official platform total has tracked around 24 million PLN by late April, while multiple press outlets and the live-stream aggregates have reported totals between roughly 100 million and just over 120 million PLN by late April to early May. The precise 124 million PLN figure is not evidenced by primary sources in the excerpts provided, and thus remains unverified. The cited entries that reflect 122 million and 120+ million totals are the closest verifications, but they do not confirm 124 million as of an official tally. In summary, the 124M claim is not supported by primary sources and should be treated as unverified until an authoritative total (Siepomaga or Cancer Fighters) confirms otherwise. The milestones and guest appearances cited (e.g., heads shaved, reconciliations, and high-profile donations) remain relevant context to understanding how totals reached the observed levels, but they do not by themselves validate the 124M figure.

One source describes the event as having reached a level that is Guinness-recordworthy, noting a Guinness World Records context in connection with the nine-day livestream and the massive funds raised. Another nearby report reiterates the Guinness framing by indicating the livestream had reached record-setting magnitude and frames Guinness as the benchmark being sought or discussed at that time. Together, these sources anchor the claim that the event was discussed in terms of a Guinness World Record for charity livestreams, even if the official confirmation from Guinness had not yet been granted. In addition to the Guinness-specific pieces, there are credible reports describing the event as achieving a high-water mark in fundraising (e.g., “Rekordowa zbi�f3rka na Fundacj99 Cancer Fighters” and related coverage), which supports the broader notion of landmark benchmarking that this field value is capturing. This combination—explicit Guinness framing and corroborating record-level fundraising reporting—provides the strongest support for the finegrained field value, while also highlighting the caveat that Guinness verification was not confirmed at the time of reporting.
